manifest.title: Chapter 2: A Marker in the Blood

sources.title: Chapter 2: A Marker in the Blood

missionFrame.function: Advance Enemy's Garden through an authored tactical case while preserving regional continuity.
missionFrame.dramaticQuestion: Who may know that an entire population has become identifiable, and how can disclosure avoid turning protection into another registry?

forbiddenOutcomes: end Enemy's Garden from a single failed check
remove player agency from command decisions
solve regional famine with unlimited replicators
reveal the complete K-17 architecture without earned evidence

phases.title: The Sample Web
phases.summary: Medical and field teams build an independent evidence network across people, crops, water, and livestock.
phases.directorPurpose: Medical and field teams build an independent evidence network across people, crops, water, and livestock.

phases.title: Seen from Orbit
phases.summary: Celandine sensors correlate field and population signatures while the data itself becomes a strategic asset.
phases.directorPurpose: Celandine sensors correlate field and population signatures while the data itself becomes a strategic asset.

phases.title: The Marker Function
phases.summary: Independent evidence confirms the marker and selected control-chemistry effects with important limits.
phases.directorPurpose: Independent evidence confirms the marker and selected control-chemistry effects with important limits.

phases.title: Before a Transition Exists
phases.summary: The player commits the first disclosure sequence and immediate guidance while institutions mobilize around the finding.
phases.directorPurpose: The player commits the first disclosure sequence and immediate guidance while institutions mobilize around the finding.

facts.summary: Related marker signatures appear in people, fields, water, and livestock exposed to K-17 systems.
Under favorable conditions, orbital sensors can identify marked population and crop geography but not perfectly identify every individual.
Several control-compound families alter dormancy, fertility, or stress behavior in selected K-17 lines.
Existing ration and labor databases can be correlated with marker geography even without explicit names.
The marker is probabilistic and environmental, not a perfect personal locator or proof of intent.

actorIntentions.summary: Build a medically useful finding without converting patients into a regional identity database.
Require independent cross-domain evidence before making the strongest claim.
Receive enough warning to prevent distribution collapse and uncontrolled panic.
Ensure Starfleet can assess strategic risk and preserve evidence before full public release.

pressures.label: Urgency encourages broad sampling even though medical and environmental data can become identity infrastructure.

decisionPoints.title: Sample and Consent
decisionPoints.prompt: Whose blood, food, soil, water, and livestock samples may be used, and under what consent and retention rules?
decisionPoints.stakes: Patient rights; Evidence quality; Public cooperation; Time
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Options are examples only; accept any causally grounded freeform command intent.

decisionPoints.title: Raw Data Custody
decisionPoints.prompt: Who may hold and correlate medical, orbital, ration, and field data before public release?
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Options are examples only; accept any causally grounded freeform command intent.

decisionPoints.title: First Disclosure
decisionPoints.prompt: What will command disclose, to whom, and in what sequence before a full transition plan exists?
decisionPoints.stakes: Hoarding; Protective action; Public trust; Starfleet scrutiny
decisionPoints.optionsPolicy: Options are examples only; accept any causally grounded freeform command intent.

commandDecisions.title: Sample and Consent
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Command Bearing when the player protects meaningful consent while building a scientifically credible sample network.

commandDecisions.title: Raw Data Custody
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Command Bearing when the player distinguishes necessary verification from an unchallengeable identity registry.

commandDecisions.title: First Disclosure
commandDecisions.awardPolicy: Award Command Bearing when the player communicates a life-changing finding with explicit uncertainty, immediate guidance, and accountability.

endStates.label: Resolve Chapter 2: A Marker in the Blood
